title: sagina rupestris
intro: Sagina rupestris, also known as rock marguerite or alpine pearlwort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Caryophyllaceae. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic activities. The health benefits of Sagina rupestris include its potential to treat various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its effectiveness and safety in these forms may require further research and clinical validation.
